pageTitle: Ask the court to set aside (cancel) the order
caption: Make an application
heading: Ask the court to set aside (cancel) the order
paragraph1: You can ask the court to set aside (cancel) the order if you have good reason. For example, if you were unable to attend the original court hearing, because you were ill.
paragraph2: You will need to show that you have a good reason to ask the court to set aside the order, and you may not be successful.
ifYouCanPayTheCourtFee: if you can pay the court fee, or if you need help with fees (help to pay court fees)
whyYouAreAskingTheCourt: why you are asking the court to set aside the original decision
youMayNeedToProvideEvidence: You may need to provide some evidence to the court.
beforeYouStart: Before you start: Make sure that you have all of the evidence you need to apply.
onceYouStart: Once you start your application, you cannot save your progress as a draft.
weWillNotSaveYourAnswers: This means that if you leave your application before submitting, we will not save your answers so that you can return to them later.
usualCosts: It usually costs {{standardFee, currency(currency: GBP; trailingZeroDisplay: stripIfInteger)}} to apply. The fee will increase to {{maxFee, currency(currency: GBP; trailingZeroDisplay: stripIfInteger)}} if:
youHaveAlreadyToldTheOtherParty: you have already told the other party that you are making this application, and
theyDidNotAgreeToIt: they did not agree to it (this means that they have objected to it)
youWillSeeTheFinalApplicationFee: You’ll see the final application fee before you pay.
ifYouAreWorriedAboutPayingFees: If you are worried about paying fees
youMayBeEligibleForHWF: You may be eligible to apply for help with fees (GOV.UK, opens in a new tab). We will ask you if you want to do this when you apply.
ifPreferToRespondByPost: If you’d prefer to respond by post:
sendTheCompletedFormToTheCourt: send the completed form to the court
